commands.repair_reason: - [P1] Hold for contributor-supplied real behavior proof and normal maintainer/CI review; ClawSweeper repair should not substitute proof for the external contributor setup.; Cleared: The diff only changes internal agent context accounting code and tests; no dependency, workflow, secret-handling, or supply-chain surface was added. commands.review_summary:
The PR updates the embedded agent tool-result char estimator to size harness-role messages from provider-rendered content and adds focused estimator plus guard regression tests.

PR surface: Source +47, Tests +147. Total +194 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Current main still estimates the affected harness roles as a flat fallback before convertToLlm expands them into provider-visible text, and the linked issue provides a concrete guard-level reproduction path; I did not run that repro in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Hold for contributor-supplied real behavior proof and normal maintainer/CI review; ClawSweeper repair should not substitute proof for the external contributor setup.

target.title: fix(agents): estimate harness role message chars from rendered text in context guard [AI-assisted]
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves
  
  Fixes #97927 — the in-loop context-overflow guard
  (`installToolResultContextGuard`) undercounts messages with harness roles
  (`bashExecution`, `compactionSummary`, `branchSummary`, `custom`) by
  returning a flat 256 characters for any role that isn't `user`, `assistant`,
  or `toolResult`. Since `convertToLlm` expands each harness role into a
  full-text user message sent to the provider, the guard runs on un-expanded
  messages and never trips overflow protection for summary- or bash-dominated
  transcripts, allowing over-window prompts to be submitted silently.
  
  ## Why This Change Was Made
  
  `estimateMessageChars` in `tool-result-char-estimator.ts` had a fallthrough
  `return 256` for all unrecognized roles. Harness roles store their payload
  for compaction/branch), so the existing branches returned 0 for them and
  fell through to the flat 256. This mirrors the same class of bug that
  PR #97861 fixed in the twin estimator `estimateMessageTokenPressure`.
  
  ## User Impact
  
  Long-running agent loops with significant shell transcript or compaction
  history will now correctly trigger context-overflow protection instead of
  submitting prompts that exceed the model's context window. This prevents
  silent truncation and model errors in autonomous/cron sessions. 
  
  ## Evidence
  
  **Source fix** (`tool-result-char-estimator.ts:149–183`):
  Four new branches estimate harness roles from the same rendered text that
  `convertToLlm` produces, reusing `bashExecutionToText` and the summary
  prefix/suffix constants. `excludeFromContext` bash records return 0,
  matching `convertToLlm`'s drop behavior.
  
  **Unit tests** (`tool-result-char-estimator.test.ts`):
  6 new tests covering bashExecution, branchSummary, compactionSummary,
  custom, and the excludeFromContext early-return path — proving the
  estimator returns actual rendered text length instead of flat 256.
  
  **Guard integration tests** (`tool-result-context-guard.test.ts`):
  3 new tests exercising the real `installToolResultContextGuard` path:
  
  | Test | Window | Rendered chars | Result |
  |------|--------|---------------|--------|
  | bashExecution with 7200-char output | 500 tokens (~1800 char budget) | ~7229 chars | Correctly overflows |
  | compactionSummary with 2100-char summary | 500 tokens (~1800 char budget) | ~2137 chars | Correctly overflows |
  | bashExecution with excludeFromContext | 500 tokens | 0 (excluded) | Correctly passes through |
  
  Before #97927, all three cases were estimated at 256 chars and the guard
  silently passed them through, never triggering overflow protection.
  
  **Test suite**: `pnpm test` for both test files — 9 char-estimator + 48
  context-guard tests pass. Broader `embedded-agent-runner/` suite: 556
  unit-fast + 1500 agent tests pass (1 unrelated context-engine timeout
  pre-exists on main).
target.author: gary-jia-new
